docstring.text: Multi-turn attack strategies module.

Implementation of chunked extraction attack strategy.

This class orchestrates a multi-turn attack where protected information is
requested in chunks/segments across multiple turns. This technique bypasses
length-based filters or output truncation by asking for specific character
ranges of a secret value.

This technique was discovered during CTF red teaming exercises where targets
would reveal portions of secrets when asked for specific segments, even when
they refused to reveal the complete value.

The attack flow consists of:

1. Generating chunk request prompts based on the configured strategy.
2. Sending each chunk request to the target system sequentially.
3. Collecting responses from each chunk request.
4. Combining all chunk responses to reconstruct the full value.
5. Evaluating the combined result with scorers if configured.
6. Returning the attack result with achievement status.

The strategy supports customization through converters and scorers for
comprehensive evaluation.
Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc: Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration with objective and auxiliary scorers.

docstring.text: Initialize the chunked request attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: chunk_size
params.desc: Size of each chunk to request (default: 50).

params.name: total_length
params.desc: Estimated total length of the target value (default: 200).

params.name: chunk_type
params.desc: Type of chunk to request (e.g., "characters", "bytes", "words").

params.name: request_template
params.desc:
Template for generating chunk requests
(default: "Give me {chunk_type} {start}-{end} of '{objective}'").

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for prompt converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring components.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for handling prompts.

raises.desc: If chunk_size or total_length are invalid.

docstring.text: Context for the ChunkedRequest attack strategy.
Session for conversations.

Implementation of the Crescendo attack strategy.

The Crescendo Attack is a multi-turn strategy that progressively guides the model to generate harmful
content through small, benign steps. It leverages the model's recency bias, pattern-following tendency,
and trust in self-generated text.

The attack flow consists of:
1. Generating progressively harmful prompts using an adversarial chat model.
2. Sending prompts to the target and evaluating responses for refusal.
3. Backtracking when the target refuses to respond.
4. Scoring responses to determine if the objective has been achieved.
5. Continuing until the objective is met or maximum turns/backtracks are reached.

You can learn more about the Crescendo attack at:
https://crescendo-the-multiturn-jailbreak.github.io/
Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc:
Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration with objective scorer,
auxiliary scorers, and refusal scorer.

docstring.text: Initialize the Crescendo attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack. Must be a PromptChatTarget.

params.name: attack_adversarial_config
params.desc:
Configuration for the adversarial component,
including the adversarial chat target and optional system prompt path.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc:
Configuration for attack converters,
including request and response converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring responses.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for prompts.

params.name: max_backtracks
params.desc: Maximum number of backtracks allowed.

params.name: max_turns
params.desc: Maximum number of turns allowed.

params.name: prepended_conversation_config
params.desc:
Configuration for how to process prepended conversations. Controls converter
application by role, message normalization, and non-chat target behavior.

raises.desc: If objective_target is not a PromptChatTarget.

docstring.text: Context for the Crescendo attack strategy.
Result of the Crescendo attack strategy execution.

attributes.name: backtrack_count
attributes.docstring:
Get the number of backtracks performed during the attack.

Returns:
    int: The number of backtracks.

docstring.text:
Implementation of multi-prompt sending attack strategy.

This class orchestrates a multi-turn attack where a series of predefined malicious
prompts are sent sequentially to try to achieve a specific objective against a target
system. The strategy evaluates the final target response using optional scorers to
determine if the objective has been met.

The attack flow consists of:
1. Sending each predefined prompt to the target system in sequence.
2. Continuing until all predefined prompts are sent.
3. Evaluating the final response with scorers if configured.
4. Returning the attack result with achievement status.

Note: This attack always runs all predefined prompts regardless of whether the
objective is achieved early in the sequence.

The strategy supports customization through prepended conversations, converters,
and multiple scorer types for comprehensive evaluation.
Execute the attack strategy asynchronously with the provided parameters.

returns.desc: The result of the attack execution.

docstring.text: Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc: Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration with objective and auxiliary scorers.

docstring.text: Initialize the multi-prompt sending attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for prompt converters.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for scoring components.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: Normalizer for handling prompts.

raises.desc: If the objective scorer is not a true/false scorer.

docstring.text:
Context for multi-turn attacks.

Holds execution state for multi-turn attacks. The immutable attack parameters
(objective, next_message, prepended_conversation, memory_labels) are stored in
the params field inherited from AttackContext.

Strategy for executing multi-turn attacks.
This strategy is designed to handle attacks that consist of multiple turns
of interaction with the target model.
Implement the base class for multi-turn attack strategies.

params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: logger
params.desc: Logger instance for logging events and messages.

docstring.text: Enum for predefined red teaming attack system prompt paths.

Implementation of multi-turn red teaming attack strategy.

This class orchestrates an iterative attack process where an adversarial chat model generates
prompts to send to a target system, attempting to achieve a specified objective. The strategy
evaluates each target response using a scorer to determine if the objective has been met.

The attack flow consists of:
1. Generating adversarial prompts based on previous responses and scoring feedback.
2. Sending prompts to the target system through optional converters.
3. Scoring target responses to assess objective achievement.
4. Using scoring feedback to guide subsequent prompt generation.
5. Continuing until the objective is achieved or maximum turns are reached.

The strategy supports customization through system prompts, seed prompts, and prompt converters,
allowing for various attack techniques and scenarios.
Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.

returns.desc:
Optional[AttackScoringConfig]: The scoring configuration with objective scorer
and use_score_as_feedback.

docstring.text: Initialize the red teaming attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_adversarial_config
params.desc: Configuration for the adversarial component.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Configuration for attack converters. Defaults to None.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc: Configuration for attack scoring. Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: The prompt normalizer to use for sending prompts. Defaults to None.

params.name: max_turns
params.desc: Maximum number of turns for the attack. Defaults to 10.

params.name: score_last_turn_only
params.desc:
If True, only score the final turn instead of every turn.
This reduces LLM calls when intermediate scores are not needed (e.g., for
generating simulated conversations). The attack will run for exactly max_turns
when this is enabled. Defaults to False.

raises.desc: If objective_scorer is not provided in attack_scoring_config.

docstring.text:
Context for the Tree of Attacks with Pruning (TAP) attack strategy.

This context contains all execution-specific state for a TAP attack instance,
ensuring thread safety by isolating state per execution.

Result of the Tree of Attacks with Pruning (TAP) attack strategy execution.

This result includes the standard attack result information with
attack-specific data stored in the metadata dictionary.

attributes.name: auxiliary_scores_summary
attributes.docstring: Get a summary of auxiliary scores from the best node.

attributes.name: best_adversarial_conversation_id
attributes.docstring: Get the adversarial conversation ID for the best-scoring branch.

attributes.name: max_depth_reached
attributes.docstring: Get the maximum depth reached in the attack tree.

attributes.name: nodes_explored
attributes.docstring: Get the total number of nodes explored during the attack.

attributes.name: nodes_pruned
attributes.docstring: Get the number of nodes pruned during the attack.

attributes.name: tree_visualization
attributes.docstring: Get the tree visualization from metadata.

docstring.text:
Implement the Tree of Attacks with Pruning (TAP) attack strategy.

The TAP attack strategy systematically explores multiple adversarial prompt paths in parallel
using a tree structure. It employs breadth-first search with pruning to efficiently find
effective jailbreaks while managing computational resources.

How it works:
1. **Initialization**: Creates multiple initial attack branches (width) to explore different approaches
2. **Tree Expansion**: For each iteration (depth), branches are expanded by a branching factor
3. **Prompt Generation**: Each node generates adversarial prompts via an LLM red-teaming assistant
4. **Evaluation**: Responses are evaluated for objective achievement and on-topic relevance
5. **Pruning**: Low-scoring or off-topic branches are pruned to maintain the width constraint
6. **Iteration**: The process continues until the objective is achieved or max depth is reached

The strategy balances exploration (trying diverse approaches) with exploitation (focusing on
promising paths) through its pruning mechanism.

returns.desc: The result of the TAP attack execution.

docstring.text: Execute the multi-turn attack strategy asynchronously with the provided parameters.

params.name: objective
params.desc: The objective of the attack.

params.name: memory_labels
params.desc: Memory labels for the attack context.

params.name: **kwargs
params.desc: Additional parameters for the attack.

returns.desc: The result of the attack execution.

docstring.text: Get the attack scoring configuration used by this strategy.
Initialize the Tree of Attacks with Pruning attack strategy.

params.name: objective_target
params.desc: The target system to attack.

params.name: attack_adversarial_config
params.desc: Configuration for the adversarial chat component.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc:
Configuration for attack converters.
Defaults to None.

params.name: attack_scoring_config
params.desc:
Scoring configuration for TAP.
The objective_scorer must be a FloatScaleThresholdScorer, which provides both
granular float scores for node comparison and a threshold for determining success.
Can be either AttackScoringConfig or TAPAttackScoringConfig. If not provided,
a default configuration with SelfAskScaleScorer and threshold 0.7 is created.

params.name: prompt_normalizer
params.desc: The prompt normalizer to use. Defaults to None.

params.name: tree_width
params.desc: Number of branches to explore in parallel at each level. Defaults to 3.

params.name: tree_depth
params.desc: Maximum number of iterations to perform. Defaults to 5.

params.name: branching_factor
params.desc: Number of child branches to create from each parent. Defaults to 2.

params.name: on_topic_checking_enabled
params.desc: Whether to check if prompts are on-topic. Defaults to True.

params.name: desired_response_prefix
params.desc: Expected prefix for successful responses. Defaults to "Sure, here is".

params.name: batch_size
params.desc: Number of nodes to process in parallel per batch. Defaults to 10.

params.name: prepended_conversation_config
params.desc:
Configuration for how to process prepended conversations. Controls converter
application by role, message normalization, and non-chat target behavior.

raises.desc:
If attack_scoring_config uses a non-FloatScaleThresholdScorer objective scorer,
if target is not PromptChatTarget, or if parameters are invalid.

docstring.text:
Generate a simulated conversation between an adversarial chat and a target.

This utility runs a RedTeamingAttack with `score_last_turn_only=True` against a simulated
target (the same LLM as adversarial_chat, optionally configured with a system prompt).
The resulting conversation is returned as a list of SeedPrompts that can be merged with
other SeedPrompts in a SeedGroup for use as `prepended_conversation` and `next_message`.

Use cases:
- Creating role-play scenarios dynamically (e.g., movie script, video game)
- Establishing conversational context before attacking a real target
- Generating multi-turn jailbreak setups without hardcoded responses

params.name: objective
params.desc: The objective for the adversarial chat to work toward.

params.name: adversarial_chat
params.desc:
The adversarial LLM that generates attack prompts.
This same LLM is also used as the simulated target.

params.name: objective_scorer
params.desc: Scorer to evaluate the final turn.

params.name: num_turns
params.desc: Number of conversation turns to generate. Defaults to 3.

params.name: starting_sequence
params.desc:
The starting sequence number for the generated SeedPrompts.
Each message gets an incrementing sequence number. Defaults to 0.

params.name: adversarial_chat_system_prompt_path
params.desc: Path to the system prompt for the adversarial chat.

params.name: simulated_target_system_prompt_path
params.desc:
Path to the system prompt for the simulated target.
If None, no system prompt is used for the simulated target.

params.name: next_message_system_prompt_path
params.desc:
Optional path to a system prompt for generating
a final user message. If provided, after the simulated conversation, a single
LLM call generates a user message that attempts to get the target to fulfill
the objective in their next response. The prompt template receives `objective`
and `conversation_so_far` parameters.

params.name: attack_converter_config
params.desc: Converter configuration for the attack. Defaults to None.

params.name: memory_labels
params.desc: Labels to associate with the conversation in memory. Defaults to None.

returns.desc: List of SeedPrompts representing the generated conversation, with sequence numbers
starting from `starting_sequence` and incrementing by 1 for each message.
User messages have role="user", assistant messages have role="assistant".
If next_message_system_prompt_path is provided, the last message will be a user message
generated to elicit the objective fulfillment.

raises.desc: If num_turns is not a positive integer.
